root_cause: Newly provisioned nodes failed to join the cluster, leaving pods Pending
evidence: Scheduler reported insufficient capacity: 0/12 nodes are available: 12 Insufficient cpu, 12 Insufficient memory.
Cluster autoscaler started node provisioning: pod triggered scale-up: [{prod-workers 12->13 (max: 20)}]
Node provisioning failed to join: Node ip-10-41-18-203.ec2.internal is not ready: kubelet never posted node status
Cluster autoscaler status reports unregistered or NotReady nodes
Provisioned node readiness signal: ip-10-41-18-203.ec2.internal Ready=False reason=NodeStatusNeverUpdated
No successful Scheduled event observed after node provisioning failure

causes.message: Scheduler could not place the Pod on existing nodes because cluster capacity was insufficient
Cluster autoscaler requested or started provisioning additional node capacity
A newly provisioned node failed to register or become Ready in the cluster
Pod remains Pending because replacement node capacity never became schedulable

pod:payments-worker-5f87bc9f6c-rx7tp: Pod is Pending after autoscaler provisioning started but new node capacity did not join
Scheduler message: 0/12 nodes are available: 12 Insufficient cpu, 12 Insufficient memory.
controller:cluster-autoscaler: NodeNotReady: Node ip-10-41-18-203.ec2.internal is not ready: kubelet never posted node status

Cluster-autoscaler status at 2026-05-16 12:20:00 +0000 UTC: ScaleUp: Backoff (unregistered nodes present: ip-10-41-18-203.ec2.internal did not register within max node provision time); NodeGroups: prod-workers currentSize=13 targetSize=13
configmap:cluster-autoscaler-status: Cluster-autoscaler status at 2026-05-16 12:20:00 +0000 UTC: ScaleUp: Backoff (unregistered nodes present: ip-10-41-18-203.ec2.internal did not register within max node provision time); NodeGroups: prod-workers currentSize=13 targetSize=13

likely_causes: Node bootstrap or kubelet registration failed before the node became Ready
Cloud-init, startup scripts, or node image configuration prevented kubelet from joining the cluster
Network, IAM, TLS bootstrap, or CSR approval problems blocked node registration
Cluster autoscaler requested nodes, but the new capacity never became schedulable
suggested_checks: kubectl -n kube-system get configmap cluster-autoscaler-status -o yaml
Inspect cloud-init, kubelet, bootstrap, and node registration logs for the failed instance
Check node IAM/identity, network reachability to the API server, and pending CSRs
